breaking_api_changes: NewSnapshot(cat, vw, fileID) -> NewSnapshot(cat, vw, em, fileID)
SnapshotRead(cat, vw, fileID) -> SnapshotRead(cat, vw, em, fileID)
VersionWriter.CommitVersion(cat, w, fileID, data) -> CommitVersion(cat, w, em, fileID, data)
Snapshot gains a Close() method; every existing call site that creates a Snapshot (directly or via SnapshotRead) must now release its epoch reference
no_external_callers: grep across the whole repo (outside engine/mvcc) found zero call sites for NewVersionWriter/NewSnapshot/SnapshotRead/CommitVersion, so this breaking change is confined to engine/mvcc's own test files
new_public_api: func RunCompaction(cat *catalog.Catalog, vw *VersionWriter, em *EpochManager, fileID uint64) ([]uint64, error)
risk: medium — touches the hot commit/read path wiring from a previously-standalone primitive; mitigated by the existing 2a.1.* test suite (TestSnapshotRead, TestSnapshotReadNoVersionCommitted, TestConcurrentReadersWriters) all still exercising the same behavior post-wiring, plus TestEpochRefcount*/TestCompactor for the epoch bookkeeping itself
